article_items.title: NEA’s Tiffany Luck says enterprises are still figuring out their AI ROI | TechCrunch
article_items.description: What will it take for personal AI agents to finally click? NEA partner Tiffany Luck joins Equity to discuss that, this year's AI IPOs, and the startups helping enterprises track their AI spend.
article_items.excerpt: What will it take for personal AI agents to finally click? NEA partner Tiffany Luck joins Equity to discuss that, this year's AI IPOs, and the startups helping enterprises track their AI spend.
article_items.summary: SpaceX to acquire Cursor for $60B in stock, days after blockbuster IPO The US government’s Anthropic models ban was never about an AI jailbreak Amazon CEO reportedly raised Anthropic model concerns before government crackdown The FBI built its own replica small town to simulate real-world cyberattacks Meta’s months-old AI unit is a soul-crushing gulag, say the engineers stuck inside it Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world Every weekday and Sunday, you. On this episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast, Luck joins Rebecca Bellan to talk about the future of personal agents, her thoughts on this year’s AI IPOs, and how startups are stepping in to help enterprises track return on AI spend. Through candid fireside chats and high-impact networking, you’ll walk away with valuable insights and new connections. She got her start convincing companies that e-commerce was the future, and now she’s all in on AI, especially when it comes to the possibilities for “magic moments” in the consumer business. article_items.description: Snap's long-awaited smart glasses debut hasn't exactly done wonders for the company's stock.

article_items.title: At Minnesota Capitol, pushback from unions, industry halted new regulations on massive data centers
article_items.description: Opponents hoped the Minnesota Legislature would approve new oversight of large data centers this year, but those bills never gained momentum. Lawmakers adjourned last month without passing any new restrictions.
article_items.excerpt: Opponents hoped the Minnesota Legislature would approve new oversight of large data centers this year, but those bills never gained momentum. Lawmakers adjourned last month without passing any new restrictions.

article_items.title: OpenAI works to stop ChatGPT generating 'sex crime scene' images
article_items.description: But researchers say its still possible to trick the AI chatbot into producing graphic content.
article_items.excerpt: But researchers say its still possible to trick the AI chatbot into producing graphic content.
article_items.summary: The AI Security Institute would continue to work with developers to quickly strengthen security before models are released, it added. "After investigating this trend, we've introduced additional safeguards against this type of prompt," it said in a statement. The researchers first alerted OpenAI in May and shared their findings, but received only an automated response from the tech company. Porn company fined £600,000 by Ofcom over age check failings Ofcom said Youngtek Solutions Ltd did not have checks between July and September 2025, but has since brought in verification. Jim Nightingale, the firm's AI safety and security researcher who uncovered the issues, said he was left "shaken, and in tears" by the images the chatbot could be made to generate.
